estado.title: Scraper status
estado.subtitle: Live monitor of which exchange houses we are collecting correctly.
estado.loadError: We couldn't load the status right now.
estado.methodologyText: We cross three API signals: whether the scraper ran without errors, whether it actually returned today's quotes, and each row's timestamp. That lets us tell a hard failure (a run error) from a silent one (ran but parsed nothing, e.g. a site that changed its structure).
estado.seoTitle: Exchange house scraper status | Cambio Uruguay
estado.seoDescription: Live monitor of quote-collection status for every exchange house in Uruguay: which work, which fail, plus USD market insights.

faq.metaTitle: FAQ about the dollar and exchange rates in Uruguay

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.intro: Answers to the most common questions about exchange rates in Uruguay, with real-time data from over 40 exchange houses.

pillar.aboutTitle: Everything About the Dollar Exchange Rate in Uruguay
pillar.aboutDescription: The US dollar is the most traded currency in Uruguay. Its exchange rate directly impacts the country's economy, from import prices to family savings. At Cambio Uruguay, we offer you the most comprehensive tool to track exchange rates in real time.
pillar.howToCompareTitle: How to Compare Exchange Rates Smartly?
pillar.howToCompareDescription: Not all exchange houses offer the same rate. The difference between the best and worst can mean significant savings, especially for large amounts. Our platform automatically compares more than 40 exchange houses to show you where to get the best deal.
pillar.marketOverviewTitle: Overview of the Uruguayan Currency Market
pillar.marketOverviewDescription: Uruguay has a diverse and competitive currency market, regulated by the Central Bank of Uruguay (BCU). With more than 40 active exchange houses, public and private banks, and digital platforms like eBROU and Prex, Uruguayans have multiple options for exchanging currencies.
pillar.tipsTitle: Tips for Getting the Best Exchange Rate
pillar.tip1: Always compare before exchanging: the difference between houses can be significant
pillar.tip2: Digital platforms like eBROU usually offer preferential exchange rates
pillar.tip3: Avoid exchanging at airports or tourist areas where rates are less favorable
pillar.tip4: For large amounts, negotiate directly with the exchange house for a better rate
pillar.branchesLink: Find exchange house branches near you
pillar.advancedLink: Use the advanced comparator with detailed filters
pillar.indicatorsLink: Value of the UI, UR and BPC today

guias.metaTitle: Guides on the dollar and exchange houses in Uruguay | Cambio Uruguay
guias.metaDescription: Practical guides to buy dollars at the best price in Uruguay, understand BILLETE, CABLE and TRANSFERENCIA, and pick the best time to exchange currency. Data from over 40 exchange houses.
guias.ctaText: Compare the dollar rate across more than 40 exchange houses and decide with data, not hunches.

acerca.h1: About Cambio Uruguay: methodology and sources
acerca.intro: How we work, where the data comes from, and who is behind Uruguay's dollar rate comparator.
acerca.metaTitle: About Cambio Uruguay: methodology, sources and team | Cambio Uruguay
acerca.metaDescription: Learn the methodology of Cambio Uruguay: data from the Central Bank of Uruguay (BCU) and over 40 exchange houses, updated roughly every 10 minutes. Independent, free and transparent.
acerca.typeBillete: BILLETE: cash dollars, the physical bills you withdraw or hand over at the exchange house.
acerca.typeCable: CABLE: dollars transferred to or from abroad; applies to international operations.
acerca.typeTransferencia: TRANSFERENCIA: dollars moved electronically between accounts, without handling cash.
acerca.typeInterbancario: INTERBANCARIO: wholesale rate between banks; a market reference, not a price for the public.
acerca.interbankNote: The INTERBANCARIO type is a wholesale reference between banks. It is not a price the public can buy or sell at: for your real operations, look at BILLETE, CABLE or TRANSFERENCIA.
acerca.disclaimerText: The information on Cambio Uruguay is informational and educational. It does not constitute financial, tax or investment advice, nor a recommendation to buy or sell currency. Always verify the rate and conditions directly with the exchange house before operating.
acerca.ctaText: Check the dollar rate across more than 40 exchange houses, or dig deeper with our guides.
acerca.embedText: Have a website or blog? Add the live dollar rate with our widget. Copy this code and paste it into your page:
acerca.mcpText: Use Claude, Cursor or another AI assistant? Our free, open-source MCP server gives them live access to the exchange market: rates, best house, conversions, history and news. Add it via the hosted URL or run it with npx.

porQueDolar.metaTitle: Why is the dollar rising or falling in Uruguay? Analysis and correlation
porQueDolar.metaDescription: What moves the dollar in Uruguay: real correlation with the Brazilian real, the global dollar and US rates, with the price jumps explained day by day.
porQueDolar.title: Why is the dollar rising or falling in Uruguay?
porQueDolar.subtitle: What factors move the exchange rate, measured with data.
porQueDolar.todayNoData: There's no data for today yet.
porQueDolar.todayTopDriver: The factor most associated with these moves is {driver}.
porQueDolar.chartTitle: Dollar exchange rate and days of biggest moves
porQueDolar.chartHint: Tap a marked point to see what happened that day.
porQueDolar.driversHint: Correlation of daily returns over the available window. Does not imply causation.
porQueDolar.timelineNoNews: No archived news for this date yet.
porQueDolar.methodologyBody: We take the daily dollar exchange rate (BROU/BCU) and compare it against indicators that tend to move the peso: the Brazilian real, a broad dollar index (FRED), the US 10-year bond yield, and the Argentine dollar. We calculate the Pearson correlation on daily returns (percentage changes), not on levels, to avoid spurious correlations. Correlation measures co-movement, not causation: a high value suggests two things move together, not that one causes the other. On some days the dollar moves due to local factors (flow, BCU decisions) with no clear external driver.
porQueDolar.sources: Sources: BROU/BCU (exchange rate), FRED (dollar index, 10-year bond), argentinadatos.com (Argentine dollar). Data as of {date}.
porQueDolar.disclaimer: Information for educational purposes only, not financial advice.

brl.pos: The dollar in Uruguay tends to rise when the Brazilian real weakens.
brl.neg: The dollar in Uruguay tends to fall when the Brazilian real weakens.
brl.weak: The Brazilian real explains little of the dollar's movement in Uruguay.

dxy.pos: When the dollar strengthens globally, the Uruguayan peso tends to weaken.
dxy.neg: When the dollar strengthens globally, the Uruguayan peso tends to strengthen.
dxy.weak: The global dollar explains little of the dollar's movement in Uruguay.

us10y.pos: Higher US 10-year bond yields tend to accompany a stronger dollar here.
us10y.neg: Higher US 10-year bond yields tend to accompany a weaker dollar here.
us10y.weak: The US 10-year bond explains little of the dollar's movement in Uruguay.

eurusd.pos: The euro and the dollar in Uruguay tend to move in the same direction.
eurusd.neg: The euro and the dollar in Uruguay tend to move in opposite directions.
eurusd.weak: The euro explains little of the dollar's movement in Uruguay.

arBlue.pos: The Argentine blue dollar moves slightly in line with the dollar in Uruguay.
arBlue.neg: The Argentine blue dollar moves slightly opposite to the dollar in Uruguay.
arBlue.weak: The Argentine blue dollar barely explains the dollar's movement in Uruguay.

arOfficial.pos: The official Argentine dollar moves slightly in line with the dollar in Uruguay.
arOfficial.neg: The official Argentine dollar moves slightly opposite to the dollar in Uruguay.
arOfficial.weak: The official Argentine dollar barely explains the dollar's movement in Uruguay.

generic.pos: This indicator tends to move in the same direction as the dollar in Uruguay.
generic.neg: This indicator tends to move in the opposite direction from the dollar in Uruguay.
generic.weak: This indicator explains little of the dollar's movement in Uruguay.
